dialogos.texto_en: (We finished breakfast and got into a taxi,\n then left for Kouri Island.)
(To be precise, Okinawa and Yagaji Island\n are connected by the Yagaji Bridge%p-2;――%p;)
(Furthermore, Yagaji Island and Kouri Island\n are connected by Kouri Bridge.)
(While enjoying the view from the Kouri Bridge\n that stretches over the sea, we safely arrived\n at the front of a building called Kouri Ocean Tower.)
Uesugi-san! Did you see it? The view from the bridge!
Oh yeah, it was astounding!
Blue sky, white sandy beach, emerald green sea,\nand the bright sun...
It truly was a breathtaking view,\njust as the rumors say.\nI'm getting excited about being in Okinawa!
Yeah! I was really surprised because I've never seen\nsuch a beautiful landscape before.
Futaro was so excited even in the taxi,\nit was quite a handful.
Well, I can understand how he feels.\nIt exceeded our expectations.
The view was so beautiful, it looked like a photograph.
I know, right! It was so unreal!
The weather is great.\nIt's a perfect day for sightseeing.
I'd love to ride a bike on that bridge. Seeing the landscape \nfrom inside the car is great, but it'd be even more satisfying\nto feel the wind while riding on it.
I don't know about bikes, but that bridge\nseems to be a popular touring spot for motorcycles,\naccording to our taxi driver.
Oh! It IS a popular spot!
Touring, eh...
I heard you can get a motorcycle license in one day\nif you study for it.
Hey, hold on. You might be able to get it in one day,\nbut you still have to ride on public roads,\nso you should practice properly.
Futaro, you're still tutoring us.
That's not the issue here!
Wait a minute!\nFutaro, you have a motorcycle license, right?
Well, I have one, but it's not a good idea to go on a solo tour\nin this situation. It would be a shame to go touring\nalone on our graduation trip, when we're all together.
Yeah, since we came all the way,\nwe should be hanging out together.
...You're right.
(Hm?\n What's wrong? Nino seems oddly dissatisfied.)
Hey%p-2;――
Well, never mind. We came all the way to Kouri Island,\nso let's enjoy it to the fullest.
O-okay.
Nino, you mentioned several times\nthat you wanted to go to Kouri Island\nafter our destination was set to Okinawa, right?
That's right.\nWhat kind of places are there on Kouri Island?
There are many power spots on Kouri Island!\nThe Heart Rock at Tinu Beach is one of them.
Heart Rock?
A rock that looks like a heart.
After all, Kouri Island is the Island of Love.\nIt was originally called 'Kuijima.'
Kuijima?
The kanji is written as 'Island of Love,'\nbut it's read as 'Kuijima.'
There's a legend,\nthat's like the Okinawan version of Adam and Eve%p-2;――
It's said that this is the place\nwhere the heavenly gods\nfirst brought down a man and a woman.
Certainly, there are similarities\nto the Adam and Eve story.
In addition to Tinu Beach, there are many other\nplaces of interest, such as Chigunu Beach, which is\nsaid to be the legendary place where the two lived.
You know a lot about it, Nino.
Hm-hmm, I researched it for this trip.\nLeave everything about Kouri Island to me.
I wish you could reflect\nthat enthusiasm in you studies...
Don't say things like that anymore.\nYou're mean.
Hey, hey, before we go to the power spot,\nlet's climb the tower over there and take a look at it!
I'm curious too.\nIf we climb up, will we see more than we did from the bridge?
Of course! That is a scenic spot\nwhere we'll be able to see the whole Kouri Bridge.
Oh, that sounds exciting!
The view from the bridge was already amazing,\nbut if we can see it all from there\nit must be an incredible sight!
And there's also a must-see tourist spot\non the rooftop there.
Really? What is it?
That's a surprise for when we get there.
There should be an automatic cart\nfrom the entrance to the observation deck.\nLet's go for it!
A cart? Sounds fun!\nLet's ride, let's ride!
Come on, Futaro!\nHurry up!
H-hey, Nino! You don't have to pull me like that!
...Nino, why don't you just say it?
Oh, is it about the motorcycle?
She must have wanted to ride together.
No matter what we say,\nNino isn't the kind of girl\nwho could be that selfish.
That's why she wanted Futaro-kun to understand her.
But, it's Futaro, after all.
Yeah, it IS Futaro-kun.
